summary: Outstanding 1973 audience tape with crystal‑clear mix and electrifying performances, highlighted by a smoking China Cat Sunflower→Other One segue, monster Playing in the Band, Big River and a stellar Loose Lucy.
review: The recording captures an exceptionally clear audience tape from ’73, balancing band and crowd ambience beautifully. The show itself is hot all night, with Jerry’s tone on point and several standout moments – a blazing China Cat Sunflower→Other One transition, a long, off‑the‑charts Playing in the Band, plus superb renditions of Big River and Loose Lucy. A handful of listeners note the mids are a touch bright and Phil’s bass (and drums) can dip into the background, but overall the performance is celebrated as one of the best from that era.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer stars (average 4.6) and enthusiastic comments about both performance and sound quality; only minor, isolated criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear mix of band and audience, crisp highs, slightly bright mids; a few listeners mention Phil’s bass and drums are recessed, but overall the tape is considered one of the best audience recordings of 1973.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > The Other One
show_quality.setlist_flow: Excellent setlist with smooth segues; the band stays hot throughout, delivering a swinging, high‑energy performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as electrified, crisp, clear and ‘on his game’ throughout the concert.
band_member_comments.Phil: Generally praised after level adjustments, though one reviewer finds his bass hard to hear in places due to mid‑range brightness.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for tight rhythmic support; highlighted in a vivid metaphor describing his part in the musical “play”.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mixed feedback – most enjoy the drumming, but one reviewer notes the drums are recessed alongside Phil’s bass.
